skill.name: caveman-stats
skill.description: Show real token usage and estimated savings for the current session. Reads directly from the Claude Code session log — no AI estimation. Triggers on /caveman-stats. Output is injected by the mode-tracker hook; the model itself does not compute the numbers.
skill.summary: Display real token usage and savings from Claude Code session logs
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: All 14 static findings are false positives. The analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown backticks as shell execution, relative path links as path traversal, and YAML syntax as cryptography. The skill only reads session log files and outputs formatted statistics — no external command execution or malicious behavior exists.

content.user_title: Track Real Token Savings from Claude Code Sessions
content.value_statement: AI coding assistants can consume significant tokens per session. This skill reads actual session logs to display real-time token counts and estimated savings versus non-caveman baselines.
actual_capabilities: Read Claude Code session log files to extract real input/output token counts
Calculate estimated token savings versus non-caveman baseline
Display formatted statistics with session turn count and percentage saved
Write lifetime savings to statusline badge file for persistent display
Inject formatted output directly via hook system (model does not compute)
limitations: Requires Claude Code session log files to exist on disk
Only works with Claude Code — other editors may not have compatible session logs
Savings estimates are projections based on typical caveman compression ratios
Statusline badge requires separate statusline configuration

use_cases.title: Monitor Session Efficiency
use_cases.description: Run /caveman-stats during or after a coding session to see exactly how many tokens were used and how much you saved compared to verbose AI responses.
use_cases.target_user: Individual developers who want to track and optimize their API costs

use_cases.title: Team Cost Analysis
use_cases.description: Share session statistics with your team to demonstrate the cumulative cost savings from using caveman-style prompting across multiple sessions.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams tracking AI tool budgets

use_cases.title: Personal Productivity Tracking
use_cases.description: Use the persistent statusline badge to see lifetime token savings at a glance, building awareness of your AI usage patterns over time.
use_cases.target_user: Power users who want visibility into their AI assistant usage

prompt_templates.title: Check Current Session Stats
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this at any point during a Claude Code session to see real-time token usage

prompt_templates.title: Review End-of-Session Summary
prompt_templates.prompt:
/caveman-stats
Tell me how the session went overall
prompt_templates.scenario: Get your stats plus a high-level summary of what was accomplished

prompt_templates.title: Request Detailed Breakdown
prompt_templates.prompt:
/caveman-stats
What files did we work on most?
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine stats display with analysis of your session activity

prompt_templates.title: Share Stats with Context
prompt_templates.prompt:
/caveman-stats
Generate a brief report of this debugging session
prompt_templates.scenario: Get your token savings alongside a summary of the work done

output_examples.output:
Session: 47 turns
Input: 12,304 tokens
Output: 3,891 tokens (caveman)
Baseline: 11,247 tokens (estimated without caveman)
Saved: 7,356 tokens (~65%)

Session: 8 turns
Input: 1,892 tokens
Output: 612 tokens (caveman)
Baseline: 1,847 tokens (estimated without caveman)
Saved: 1,235 tokens (~67%)

best_practices: Run /caveman-stats at the end of significant coding sessions to track cumulative savings over time
Enable the statusline badge to see lifetime savings persistently without running commands
Compare stats between different projects or task types to identify where caveman is most effective
anti_patterns: Do not rely on caveman-stats for precise billing — use it for estimates and awareness only
Do not run /caveman-stats excessively — each session log read is unnecessary overhead
Do not ignore high token counts — they may indicate opportunities to use more concise prompting

faq.question: How does caveman-stats work?
faq.answer: It reads the Claude Code session log file directly from disk. The numbers are real token counts from the API, not estimates made by the AI model.

faq.question: How accurate are the savings estimates?
faq.answer: The input and output tokens are exact from session logs. The baseline estimate uses typical compression ratios achieved by caveman prompting.

faq.question: Does caveman-stats work with other AI assistants?
faq.answer: It is designed specifically for Claude Code. Session log formats vary by tool.

faq.question: How do I enable the statusline badge?
faq.answer: Configure your Claude Code settings to use the caveman-statusline script. Check the caveman repo for platform-specific instructions.

faq.question: Can I export my session history?
faq.answer: Session logs are stored locally by Claude Code. caveman-stats reads them but does not export or aggregate data.

faq.question: Does this skill cost tokens to run?
faq.answer: No. The /caveman-stats command triggers hook-based output injection. The model does not compute the numbers.
